questions.question: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP (energy).

questions.question: How many chromosomes do humans have?
What is the largest organ in the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ, covering about 2 square meters.

questions.question: Which blood type is the universal donor?
questions.explanation: O negative blood can be given to anyone regardless of blood type.

questions.question: What molecule carries genetic information?
questions.explanation: DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) carries the genetic instructions for life.

questions.question: Which process do plants use to convert sunlight into energy?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis converts sunlight, water, and CO₂ into glucose and oxygen.

questions.question: How many bones are in an adult human body?
What is the smallest bone in the human body?
questions.explanation: The stapes (stirrup) in the middle ear is the smallest bone at ~3mm.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced when skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: UV-B radiation triggers Vitamin D synthesis in the skin.

questions.question: What is the fastest muscle in the human body?
questions.explanation: The orbicularis oculi (eye-blinking muscle) is the fastest muscle.

questions.question: Which organism is considered the closest living relative of humans?
questions.explanation: Bonobos and chimpanzees share about 98.7% of DNA with humans.

questions.question: What is the primary function of red blood cells?
questions.explanation: Red blood cells transport oxygen using hemoglobin.

questions.question: How many chambers does the human heart have?
questions.explanation: The human heart has 4 chambers: two atria and two ventricles.

questions.question: What is the hardest substance in the human body?
questions.explanation: Tooth enamel is the hardest substance in the human body.

questions.question: Which part of the brain controls balance and coordination?
questions.explanation: The cerebellum coordinates voluntary movements and balance.
